reasoning: Initial observation suggests a linear relationship between absorbance and the product of molar absorptivity, concentration, and path length. To confirm this, I will increase the molar absorptivity and observe the change in absorbance.
The absorbance increased proportionally when the molar absorptivity was doubled, suggesting a direct relationship. Next, I will increase the concentration to further investigate the relationship.

The absorbance has consistently increased proportionally with changes in molar absorptivity and concentration, suggesting a linear relationship. Increasing the path length will help confirm the final form of the equation.

The absorbance has increased proportionally with the product of molar absorptivity, concentration, and path length. This confirms the Beer-Lambert law, which states that absorbance (A) is equal to the product of molar absorptivity (ε), concentration (c), and path length (l).
